title: YNAB Write-Back Change-Set
description: Canonical, versioned envelope describing a set of proposed, human-approvable changes to a YNAB budget ledger. A change-set is the single contract shared by propose, approve, dry-run, apply, and audit across Sprint 4 (M4) write-back. It produces no writes by itself and never moves real money (money_movement is always false). All monetary amounts are YNAB milliunits (integers; divide by 1000 for display).

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this change-set schema (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). See changeset-contract.md for the increment rules.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp marking when the change-set was produced.

budget_id.description: YNAB budget id the entire change-set targets. Every operation's budget_id must match this.

budget_name.description: Human-readable budget name, carried for display and audit.

source.description: Provenance of the change-set: the review run id that produced it, or the literal "manual".

money_movement.description: Hard invariant: a change-set NEVER moves real money. Always false. The M4-2 write-safety guardrail asserts this before any apply runs (categorize/allocate/dedup/reconcile are ledger-only).

operations.description: Ordered list of typed operations. Apply executes them in array order.

risk.description: Blast radius of the operation. destructive operations require extra confirmation at apply time.

milliunits.description: Monetary amount in YNAB milliunits (integer; divide by 1000 for display, e.g. 250000 = $250.00). Never a pre-divided float.

operation.description: A single typed operation. Discriminated on the `type` field; exactly one subtype schema matches.

categorizeOp.description: Set or overwrite a transaction's category. Applied via ynab_update_transaction(s) (M4-6).

id.description: Stable per-operation id (UUID or deterministic key). Used for idempotent resume and audit.

budget_id.description: Budget id; must equal the envelope budget_id.

transaction_id.description: Target transaction whose category is being set.

before.description: Snapshot of the transaction's category before the change. SHOULD also carry the transaction-shape evidence (subtransactions / transfer fields, GAP-19 / #49) so the M4-6 handler and the M4-2 guardrail can refuse a split parent or a transfer leg before any tool call.

category_id.description: Current category id, or null if the transaction is uncategorized.

category_name.description: Current category name, or null if uncategorized.

subtransactions.description: Shape evidence: the transaction's subtransactions. Non-empty marks the target as a SPLIT PARENT, which the categorize handler refuses (human review only, GAP-19 / #49).

transfer_account_id.description: Shape evidence: non-null marks the target as a TRANSFER LEG, which the categorize handler and the guardrail refuse (GAP-19 / #49).

transfer_transaction_id.description: Shape evidence: non-null marks the target as a TRANSFER LEG, which the categorize handler and the guardrail refuse (GAP-19 / #49).

rationale.description: One-line human-readable reason, sourced from the review.

allocateOp.description: Set a category's budgeted amount for a given month. Applied via ynab_update_category (M4-7).

id.description: Stable per-operation id (UUID or deterministic key).

budget_id.description: Budget id; must equal the envelope budget_id.

category_id.description: Target category whose budgeted amount is being set.

month.description: Budget month as YNAB's first-of-month date, e.g. 2026-06-01.

before.description: Snapshot of the budgeted amount before the change.

budgeted.description: Current budgeted amount for the month, in milliunits (integer).

after.description: Proposed budgeted amount after the change.

budgeted.description: Proposed budgeted amount for the month, in milliunits (integer).

rationale.description: One-line human-readable reason, sourced from the review.

deleteDuplicateOp.description: Remove a duplicate transaction. Applied via ynab_delete_transaction (M4-8). Always destructive. Carries twin (surviving-pair) evidence and a full before-snapshot so the deletion is reviewable, auditable, and reversible-by-reference.

id.description: Stable per-operation id (UUID or deterministic key).

budget_id.description: Budget id; must equal the envelope budget_id.

twin.description: The surviving transaction this op is a duplicate OF — the twin that stays. Required pairing evidence: a delete op must prove which record survives so the human (and the dry-run preview) sees the pair, not just the victim. The M4-8 handler rejects any delete op missing complete twin evidence before any read or delete is attempted.

payee_name.description: Surviving twin's payee name, or null if it has no payee.

transfer_account_id.description: Shape evidence: non-null marks the twin as a TRANSFER LEG — the pair is a legitimate inflow/outflow pair, never duplicates, and the M4-8 handler hard-blocks the delete (GAP-19 / #49).

transfer_transaction_id.description: Shape evidence: non-null marks the twin as a TRANSFER LEG — hard-blocked by the M4-8 handler (GAP-19 / #49).

before.description: Full snapshot of the duplicate (victim) transaction before deletion. Carries the complete transaction state so the M4-3 audit before-snapshot — written before the irreversible delete — records everything needed to review or reconstruct the removed record. The M4-4 apply-time drift check compares the live transaction against these snapshotted fields.

amount.description: Transaction amount in milliunits (integer; negative for outflow).

payee_name.description: Payee name, or null if the transaction has no payee.

category_id.description: Category id, or null if uncategorized.

account_id.description: Account the transaction belongs to — needed to project the cleared-balance impact and to audit which account was touched.

cleared.description: Cleared status at snapshot time; determines whether the deletion changes the account's cleared balance.

import_id.description: YNAB import_id used to confirm the duplicate, if any.

transfer_account_id.description: Shape evidence: non-null marks the victim as a TRANSFER LEG. Deleting one leg of a transfer pair corrupts the linked account's ledger, so the M4-8 handler hard-blocks it before any read or delete (GAP-19 / #49).

transfer_transaction_id.description: Shape evidence: non-null marks the victim as a TRANSFER LEG — hard-blocked by the M4-8 handler (GAP-19 / #49).

after.description: Proposed post-state: the transaction is removed from the ledger.

rationale.description: One-line human-readable reason, sourced from the review.

reconcileOp.description: Mark transactions cleared/reconciled and/or reconcile an account. Applied via ynab_reconcile_account plus ynab_update_transaction(s) (M4-9).

id.description: Stable per-operation id (UUID or deterministic key).

budget_id.description: Budget id; must equal the envelope budget_id.

transaction_ids.description: Optional transactions to mark cleared/reconciled as part of this reconciliation.

before.description: Snapshot of the account/transaction state before reconciliation.

cleared_balance.description: Account cleared balance before reconciliation, in milliunits (integer).

reconciled_balance.description: Account reconciled balance before reconciliation, in milliunits (integer).
Account reconciled balance after reconciliation, in milliunits (integer).

rationale.description: One-line human-readable reason, sourced from the review.
